title: Preload landing page images on first load and reduce 'Image unavailable' errors

requirements.text: Preload landing-page images at initial site open (before the user swipes) so carousel and transformation images are already in the browser cache.

quotes: Images should pre losd itself even before swipe on even opening the website in start

acceptanceCriteria: When the landing page first renders, the app begins preloading all image URLs referenced in frontend/src/components/landing/assets.ts plus the hero image (/assets/IMG_9304.jpeg).
Preloading happens without blocking first paint: above-the-fold images load with high priority, while the remainder preload in the background (e.g., after initial render / idle time).
After initial load, swiping to the next slide does not show the loading spinner for images that have already been preloaded (except in genuine network-failure scenarios).

requirements.text: Make image loading more resilient so intermittent failures do not get stuck showing 'Image unavailable' and can recover automatically.
acceptanceCriteria: If an image fails to load in MediaFrame, perform an automatic single retry (with a cache-busting URL parameter) before showing the 'Image unavailable' state.
If the retry succeeds, the image displays normally and the error overlay does not appear.
If the retry fails, show the existing 'Image unavailable' overlay and provide a user action to retry loading the image again.

requirements.text: Optimize the static image assets used by the landing page to reduce total bytes while keeping all existing image URLs unchanged.

quotes: Optimize all static image assets used on the landing page to reduce total bytes and improve perceived load time, without breaking any existing image URLs referenced in the code.

acceptanceCriteria: All existing image paths referenced in code (e.g., /assets/IMG_9308.jpeg, /assets/quality_restoration_20260215131917797-1.jpeg) continue to work without any code changes to the URLs.
Image files in the static assets folder are recompressed/optimized to smaller sizes while maintaining acceptable visual quality.
A before/after comparison of total bytes across all referenced landing-page images shows a meaningful reduction.

constraints: Do not edit any files under frontend/src/components/ui (read-only).
Do not edit files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts(x), frontend/src/hooks/useActor.ts, or frontend/src/main.tsx (immutable paths).
Backend remains single-actor; no backend changes are required for this request.
nonGoals: Adding new backend APIs or storing images in canister state.
Introducing third-party image CDN services or external storage providers.
Changing the landing page content, copy, or adding new sections beyond what is needed for image preloading/resilience.

preferencesToAdd: Prefer aggressive perceived-performance improvements for image-heavy UI (preload + resilience) while keeping URLs stable.
